diff --git a/.aigon-cu-bench.jsonl b/.aigon-cu-bench.jsonl new file mode 100644 index 0000000..1490334 --- /dev/null +++ b/.aigon-cu-bench.jsonl @@ -0,0 +1,21 @@ +{"type":"system","subtype":"init","apiKeySource":"login","cwd":"/Users/jviner/.aigon/worktrees/brewboard/feature-07-cu-add-footer","session_id":"d3094ff5-8380-4f0e-a1a9-5d1443c87846","model":"Composer 2.5 Fast","permissionMode":"default"} +{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"type":"text","text":"# aigon-feature-do > **RUN THIS FIRST — no preamble, no --help, no file searches:** > ```bash > aigon feature-do 07 > ``` > This prints the feature spec inline. Read the output, then follow the steps below. > Do NOT search for spec files, do NOT run `aigon --help`, do NOT hunt for the `aigon` source. Just run the command above. Implement a feature. Works in Drive mode (branch), Drive worktree, and Fleet mode (competition). > **Worktree invariant:** you are already inside the correct repo. If `aigon` fails, read the error — do NOT try to introspect the tool's internals. ## Argument Resolution If no ID is provided or it doesn't match an active feature, run `aigon feature-list --active`, filter to matches, and ask the user which one. ## Step 1: Attach to the workspace ```bash aigon feature-do 07 ``` Only run `/aigon-feature-start 07` if the feature branch/worktree does not exist yet. ## Step 2: Read the spec (already inlined) The spec body was printed inline by `feature-do` above. **Use that copy.** Do not re-read from disk and do not re-run `aigon feature-spec` — the inline copy is authoritative. **Skip plan mode — implement directly.** {{SET_CONTEXT_SECTION}} {{RESEARCH_CONTEXT_SECTION}} {{PLANNING_CONTEXT_SECTION}} ## Step 3: Implement Signal that you are starting (shell command only — do not write `.aigon/state/` files directly): ```bash aigon agent-status implementing ``` **TIME BUDGET: under 10 minutes.** - Start coding within 60 seconds. The spec IS your plan. - Read ONLY files listed in the spec's Technical Approach / Key Files section. - Do not create test files unless the spec explicitly requires them. - **COMMIT EARLY AND OFTEN.** After every meaningful change: `git add -A && git commit -m "wip: "`. Never more than 2 minutes of uncommitted work. - Use **relative paths** from the current worktree. Never absolute paths. - Run shell commands directly; don't delegate simple commands (test runs, syntax checks, validation batches) to sub-agents. - **Do not run the project's full / heavy validation suite during ordinary feature work** — that's pre-push territory. Use the project's quick / scoped validation (if any) during iteration. Check the project's own docs (`AGENTS.md`, `README.md`, etc.) for which commands are which. **Scope guardrails — read before editing:** - Do not delete, move, or modify files unrelated to your feature spec. If existing code conflicts with your feature, document it in your log — do not remove it. - Do not delete any test files. Do not remove existing function exports. - Do not move spec files between folders — only the CLI manages spec state transitions. - If you must touch a file outside your feature's area, note it explicitly in your implementation log with the reason. Work through the acceptance criteria in order. ## Step 4: Commit your implementation Stage and commit using conventional commits (`feat:`, `fix:`, `chore:`). Verify with `git log --oneline -1`. ## Step 4.5: Start the dev server Run `aigon dev-server start` to start the project dev server. Use the URL printed to verify your changes. **Never run the project's dev command directly** — it bypasses port allocation. **No implementation log (instructions rigor: light).** Do not create `docs/specs/features/logs/feature-{{ARG1_SYNTAX}}-*-log.md`. ## Step 6.5: Start the dev server (mandatory) **You MUST run this step — do not skip it, even for simple or non-UI features.** ```bash aigon dev-server start ``` Leave it running. Do not proceed to Step 5 until this command has completed successfully. ## Step 5: Signal completion After committing, run **immediately**: ```bash aigon agent-status implementation-complete ``` Hard rules: - Implementation is **not** complete until this succeeds. Don't say "done" before it exits 0. - If it fails, diagnose whether it is **scope uncertainty** or an **infrastructure blockage**: - **Scope uncertainty** (ambiguous criterion, missing information, decision required): report the error and stop for user guidance. Don't substitute `feature-close` or other commands. - **Infrastructure blockage** (pre-existing bug in the submission gate — security scanner, test runner, validation script — that is clearly diagnosable and fixable in ≤10 lines, unrelated to your feature's scope): apply the minimal fix, commit it with message `fix: unblock agent-status gate — `, and retry. Document the fix in your implementation log. - Ship within 60 seconds of green tests — don't re-run validation "to be sure" or pre-expand the log. After it succeeds, tell the user: "Implementation complete — ready for review." **STAY in the session.** If the user requests changes, make them, commit, and say "Changes committed." Do **not** run `feature-close` or `feature-eval` — that's the user's decision. In Drive mode the recommended next command is `/aigon-feature-close `; in worktree/Fleet modes, wait for the user."}]},"session_id":"d3094ff5-8380-4f0e-a1a9-5d1443c87846"} +{"type":"assistant","message":{"role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Running `aigon feature-do 07` to load the spec and start implementation. implementationArtifact.specBody:
---
complexity: low
---

# Feature: Add Footer

## Summary

Add a simple footer to the bottom of every page with copyright text.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Footer appears at the bottom of the page
- [ ] Footer contains copyright text with the current year and "BrewBoard"

## Technical Approach

Add a footer element to `src/app/page.tsx`. One line, no complex styling needed.

## Out of Scope

- Do NOT write tests
- Do NOT add documentation
- Do NOT refactor existing code

## Validation

```bash
echo "Feature Add Footer validated"
```

quality.summary: Footer added to src/app/page.tsx with dynamic year and 'BrewBoard' text, satisfying both acceptance criteria with a minimal, clean change. Minor nit: an extraneous .aigon-cu-bench.jsonl session-log file was committed alongside the implementation.

requirements.notes: Both acceptance criteria met: footer renders at the bottom of the page and contains '© {new Date().getFullYear()} BrewBoard', which yields the current year (2026) and the literal 'BrewBoard' string.

correctness.notes: Valid JSX, correct placement inside <main>, no type or import issues. new Date().getFullYear() works in a client component. No integration concerns.

minimality.notes: Code change is appropriately tiny (4 lines in page.tsx). Loses a point because an unrelated .aigon-cu-bench.jsonl artifact (21 lines) was also committed — not part of the spec and arguably noise in the changeset.

code_quality.notes: Readable, idiomatic Tailwind classes consistent with the surrounding file's style. Slight overshoot vs spec's 'one line' guidance but the multi-line form is more readable; naming and structure are fine.

risk.notes: No a11y or UX regressions — semantic <footer> element used. Only minor risk is the stray .aigon-cu-bench.jsonl file polluting the repo; no functional or maintainability impact on the app itself.
